items.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal AI — Meta AI
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet:
Meta's most significant recent open-source model release is the Llama 4 herd, announced in early 2026, which marks a new era of natively multimodal AI from Meta. The Llama 4 family — including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick — are available to download on llama.com and Hugging Face, as well as across major cloud platforms and edge silicon providers. These models are pre-trained on 200 languages with 10x more multilingual tokens than Llama 3, and support open-source fine-tuning.

In April 2026

items.title: Agent-driven development in Copilot Applied Science
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: GitHub has made several major AI agent announcements for GitHub Copilot over the past year. In late 2025, GitHub announced 'Agent HQ' at GitHub Universe — a unified interface across GitHub, VS Code, mobile, and CLI for directing, monitoring, and managing AI-driven tasks, with coding agents from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cognition, xAI, and others available directly within paid Copilot subscriptions. Earlier in 2025, GitHub upgraded Copilot with a full agent mode capable of iterating on its own

items.title: Small Yet Mighty: Llama Nemotron RAG Models
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: Recent Hugging Face blog posts highlight several significant model releases in early 2026. NVIDIA has been particularly active, releasing Cosmos Reason 2 — an advanced reasoning vision-language model for physical AI that topped the Physical AI Bench and Physical Reasoning leaderboards as the #1 open model for visual understanding. NVIDIA also released Llama Nemotron RAG models (small yet powerful multimodal retrieval models) and announced DGX Spark and Reachy Mini agent systems. Hugging Face its

items.title: [2604.01487] AgentSocialBench: Evaluating Privacy Risks in Human-Centered Agentic Social Networks
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week's arXiv activity on AI agents and LLMs shows a surge of new work across three themes: agent evaluation and safety, multi-agent coordination, and planning frameworks. On the safety and evaluation front, 'AgentSocialBench' (2604.01487) introduces a benchmark for measuring privacy risks in human-centered agentic social networks, while 'From LLM Reasoning to Autonomous AI Agents: A Comprehensive Review' (2504.19678) surveys ~60 benchmarks across reasoning, autonomy, and decision-making fro
